title: Generative AI for Builders — From Prompt to Production
description: Move past prompt-fiddling. Architect retrieval, evals, guardrails, and cost controls for AI features that ship and stay shipped.

videos.title: The Builder's Mental Model for LLMs
videos.verifyPrompt: Explain in your own words why an LLM is not a database, and give one product decision that flows from that distinction.

videos.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation, Honestly
videos.verifyPrompt: Describe a citation gate for a customer-support agent over a 50k-doc base. What gets a human in the loop?

videos.title: Evals That Actually Catch Regressions
videos.verifyPrompt: Design an eval set for a summarization feature. What three metrics and what failure modes are they catching?

title: Prompt Engineering for Product Teams
description: A working PM/engineer's guide to writing, versioning, and A/B-testing prompts that survive contact with real users.

videos.title: Prompts Are Specs, Not Magic
videos.verifyPrompt: Take a vague user request and rewrite it as a prompt spec with constraints, examples, and acceptance criteria.

videos.title: Versioning & Rolling Back Prompts in Prod
videos.verifyPrompt: Sketch a prompt-versioning workflow your team would actually use. What triggers a rollback?

title: Applied Data Science — Python, Pandas, and the Job
description: The 80/20 of data work in industry: clean messy data fast, frame the question right, communicate the answer.

videos.title: Reading a Real-World Dataset Without Crying
videos.verifyPrompt: Given a CSV with mixed types, missing rows, and a malformed date column, outline your first 30 minutes of work.

videos.title: From Question to Chart in One Notebook
videos.verifyPrompt: Pick a question your team actually asks every week. Sketch the chart you would build and the columns you need.

videos.title: Stakeholder-Proof Analysis Reports
videos.verifyPrompt: Write the one-paragraph TL;DR of an analysis where the result contradicts what leadership expected.

title: Practical ML — Models You Can Defend in a Meeting
description: Pick the right model, validate it honestly, and explain it to a non-technical decision-maker without losing the room.

videos.title: Why Most ML Projects Die in Pilot
videos.verifyPrompt: Name three non-technical reasons an ML project fails to ship. Pick the one you've personally seen happen and explain why.

videos.title: Honest Validation: Beyond Train/Test Split
videos.verifyPrompt: Describe a validation strategy for a churn model trained on six months of data. What about time leakage?

title: Growth Marketing — Loops, Channels, and the Spreadsheet That Matters
description: Build a growth model for your product, find the channel that actually scales, and stop measuring vanity metrics.

videos.title: Funnels Lie. Loops Don't.
videos.verifyPrompt: Sketch the primary growth loop of a product you use daily. Name the input, the action, and the compounding output.

videos.title: Channel-Product Fit, Not Just PMF
videos.verifyPrompt: Pick a startup you admire. Argue which one channel they actually depend on, with one piece of evidence.

videos.title: Building a Defensible Growth Model in Sheets
videos.verifyPrompt: Outline the columns and assumptions of a 12-month growth model. Which assumption would scare you the most if wrong?

title: Performance Marketing — Paid Channels Without Wasting Budget
description: Meta, Google, and the ROAS math behind them. Set up clean tracking, write ads that work in 2026, and read the dashboard like an operator.

videos.title: ROAS, MER, and What Your Agency Won't Tell You
videos.verifyPrompt: Define ROAS and MER. Which one would you optimize toward for a DTC brand in month 3, and why?

videos.title: Creative Iteration as a System
videos.verifyPrompt: Lay out a weekly creative-testing cadence for a small team. How many variants and what kills a losing ad?

title: FP&A Fundamentals — The Operator's Toolkit
description: Build the three-statement model, run a budget vs actuals review, and translate finance for non-finance teams.

videos.title: The Three Statements, Connected
videos.verifyPrompt: If revenue goes up but cash goes down in the same quarter, name two non-fraudulent reasons and which statement reveals each.

videos.title: Running a Real Budget vs Actuals Review
videos.verifyPrompt: You're 30% over on one line item. Walk through your first five questions before you escalate.

title: Equity Research — Reading a Company Like an Analyst
description: Crack a 10-K in 90 minutes. Build a defendable DCF. Write the one-page pitch a portfolio manager will actually read.

videos.title: The 90-Minute 10-K
videos.verifyPrompt: Pick a public company. List the three numbers you'd extract first from the 10-K and what each one tells you.

videos.title: DCF Without Self-Deception
videos.verifyPrompt: Identify the two assumptions in any DCF that swing valuation the most. How would you sanity-check each?

title: B2B Sales — From First Call to Signed Contract
description: Run a real discovery call, qualify with MEDDIC, handle the procurement gauntlet, and close without discounting your way to a 'yes'.

videos.title: Discovery That Doesn't Sound Like Discovery
videos.verifyPrompt: Write three open-ended discovery questions for a $50k ARR SaaS deal. Why each one beats a yes/no version.

videos.title: Qualifying with MEDDIC, Practically
videos.verifyPrompt: Pick a deal you've heard about. Score it on MEDDIC. Which letter is weakest and how would you strengthen it this week?

videos.title: Handling Procurement Without Folding
videos.verifyPrompt: Procurement asks for a 20% discount. List three non-price levers you could pull instead and which one you'd lead with.

title: AE Bootcamp — Running a Real Pipeline
description: From SDR-handoff to closed-won: territory planning, forecasting, mutual close plans, and the daily ritual of a top-performing AE.

videos.title: Reading a Pipeline Honestly
videos.verifyPrompt: Why is a 4x pipeline coverage number meaningless on its own? What two cuts do you actually need to trust it?

videos.title: The Mutual Close Plan, In Practice
videos.verifyPrompt: Sketch a mutual close plan for a 6-week sales cycle. Which two milestones would you absolutely not skip?
